target.title: Notify chat when main session restart recovery cannot resume
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #85804 by sending a best-effort visible recovery notice when restart recovery cannot safely resume a chat-bound main session, then marking the stale session failed so retries do not spam.
- Issue proof: a real Telegram group setup hit Codex app-server startup retry exhaustion, typing TTL expiry, and main-session restart recovery failed with `transcript tail is not resumable`, leaving the chat silent/out of context.
- Fix proof: the PR branch's focused recovery shard validates a Telegram delivery-context session calls `message.action` with the generic "couldn't safely resume" notice before failing the stale session (`17 passed`).
- Follow-up CI repair: rebased onto current `origin/main`, aligned the static model-normalization boundary expectation, and made the gateway reload timeout test deterministic under CI.

Fixes #85804.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: A Telegram-backed main session could be interrupted by a gateway restart, fail restart recovery with `transcript tail is not resumable`, and leave the chat seeing silence/out-of-context replies. The fixed behavior is a visible generic recovery notice before the stale session is marked failed.

Real environment tested: OpenClaw `2026.5.20` incident from a real Telegram group setup, with the PR branch used for after-fix recovery-path validation. Mantis Telegram Desktop proof was requested, but the workflow requires write/maintain/admin repository permission and this contributor account is only read-authorized for that secret-bearing proof workflow. The live gateway proof below therefore supplies the transport-level evidence available from the PR branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=30000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 timeout 75s node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-support.config.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.full-core-support-boundary.config.ts
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the PR branch after the patch:

```text
Real Telegram delivery-path proof:
[main-session-restart-recovery] sent interrupted main session recovery notice: <telegram-main-session> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] marked interrupted main session failed: <telegram-main-session> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] main-session restart recovery complete: recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0
first pass:  recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0, statusAfterFirst=failed
second pass: recovered=0 failed=0 skipped=0, statusAfterSecond=failed
```

This proof used a throwaway seeded stale main session with persisted Telegram delivery context, then called the real gateway `message.action` transport path from the PR branch. The generic recovery notice was delivered once to the active Telegram group; the second recovery pass did not send again because the session was already marked failed.

Focused validation:

```text
main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  17 passed (17)

plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  1 passed (1)

server.reload.test.ts:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  14 passed (14)

full-core-support-boundary:
Test Files  141 passed | 1 skipped (142)
Tests  1529 passed | 7 skipped (1536)
```

The regression validates the after-fix runtime branch for a Telegram group session with persisted delivery context: recovery calls `message.action` with `action: "send"`, `to`, `threadId`, `accountId`, `sessionKey`, and the generic "couldn't safely resume" notice before marking the interrupted session failed.

Before evidence optional: Redacted runtime log excerpt from the real setup before the fix:

```text
[agent/embedded] codex app-server connection closed during startup; retries exhausted
[agents/harness] Codex agent harness failed; not falling back to embedded PI backend
[diagnostic] lane task error: lane=main durationMs=<duration> error="Error: codex app-server client is closed"
Embedded agent failed before reply: codex app-server client is closed
[typing] TTL exceeded, auto-stopping typing indicator
[main-session-restart-recovery] marked interrupted main session failed: <telegram-main-session> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] main-session restart recovery complete: recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0
```

Observed result after fix: The patched path emits the visible recovery notice call for chat-bound unresumable sessions and still marks the stale interrupted session failed so later restart-recovery retries do not spam the chat.

What was not tested: I did not intentionally crash a live production gateway after installing this branch, because that would risk disrupting active sessions. The after-fix proof above exercises the real Telegram send path from a throwaway seeded recovery session. Mantis Telegram Desktop proof could not be captured from this contributor account because the workflow requires write/maintain/admin repository permission.


### Live Telegram gateway proof, May 23 2026

I also exercised the patched recovery path against the real gateway transport from this PR branch with a throwaway seeded main session whose persisted delivery context pointed at the active Telegram proof chat. This intentionally sent the generic recovery notice once to Telegram, then immediately reran recovery against the same seeded state to prove no repeat after the session was marked failed.

Exact command shape, with private chat/session details redacted:

\`\`\`bash
timeout 30s node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'PROOF_EOF'
# Seed /tmp/openclaw-pr85805-proof-*/agents/main/sessions/sessions.json with:
# - status: "running"
# - abortedLastRun: true
# - deliveryContext: { channel: "telegram", to: "<redacted-proof-chat>", accountId: "default" }
# Seed pr85805-proof-session.jsonl with a user message followed by an assistant partial,
# then call recoverRestartAbortedMainSessions({ stateDir: root }) twice.
PROOF_EOF
\`\`\`

Redacted terminal output from the PR branch:

\`\`\`text
starting proof
[main-session-restart-recovery] sent interrupted main session recovery notice: agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted-proof-chat> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] marked interrupted main session failed: agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted-proof-chat> (transcript tail is not resumable)
[main-session-restart-recovery] main-session restart recovery complete: recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0
PR #85805 live gateway Telegram proof (redacted)
stateDir=<tmp>/openclaw-pr85805-proof-XuaLdD
sessionKey=agent:main:telegram:group:<redacted-proof-chat>
first pass:  recovered=0 failed=1 skipped=0, statusAfterFirst=failed
second pass: recovered=0 failed=0 skipped=0, statusAfterSecond=failed
exit:0
\`\`\`

Observed result: the generic recovery notice appeared once in the Telegram proof chat via the real gateway message.action transport. The second pass did not send again because the seeded session was already persisted as failed.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/main-session-restart-recovery.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-server.config.ts src/gateway/server.reload.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.full-core-support-boundary.config.ts`
target.author: pfrederiksen
